def solve(nums)
  n = nums.length
  if n <= 1
    return nums.dup

    l_max[i] = current_max
  end

  # Step 2: Calculate RMax[i] = max(nums[i+1...n-1]). Use -1 as sentinel.
  r_max = Array.new(n)
  r_max[n - 1] = -1
  current_max = -1
  # Iterate backwards from n-2 down to 0
  for i in (0...(n - 1)).to_a.reverse
    current_max = [current_max, nums[i+1]].max
    r_max[i] = current_max
  end

  # Step 3: Identify Valid Elements
  result = []
  for i in 0...n
    is_valid = (nums[i] > l_max[i]) || (nums[i] > r_max[i])
    if is_valid
      result << nums[i]
    end
  end

  return result
end

def solve(nums)
    n = nums.length
    return nums.dup if n <= 1

    # L[i] is true if nums[i] is strictly greater than all elements to its left.
    # L[0] is always true.
    l_greater = Array.new(n, false)
    current_max = -1
    for i in 0...n
        if i == 0
            l_greater[i] = true
        else
            if nums[i] > current_max
                l_greater[i] = true
            end
        end
        current_max = [current_max, nums[i]].max
    end

    # R[i] is true if nums[i] is strictly greater than all elements to its right.
    # R[n-1] is always true.
    r_greater = Array.new(n, false)
    current_max = -1
    for i in (0...n).to_a.reverse
        if i == n - 1
            r_greater[i] = true
        else
            if nums[i] > current_max
                r_greater[i] = true
            end
        end
        current_max = [current_max, nums[i]].max
    end

    result = []
    for i in 0...n
        # An element is valid if it satisfies the left condition OR the right condition.
        # Since L[0]=T and R[n-1]=T, the boundary conditions are covered.
        if l_greater[i] || r_greater[i]
            result << nums[i]
        end
    end

    return result
end

def solve(nums)
    n = nums.length
    return nums.dup if n <= 1

    # L[i] is true if nums[i] is strictly greater than all elements to its left.
    # L[0] is always true.
    l_greater = Array.new(n, false)
    current_max = -1
    for i in 0...n
        if i == 0
            l_greater[i] = true
        else
            if nums[i] > current_max
                l_greater[i] = true
            end
        end
        current_max = [current_max, nums[i]].max
    end

    # R[i] is true if nums[i] is strictly greater than all elements to its right.
    # R[n-1] is always true.
    r_greater = Array.new(n, false)
    current_max = -1
    for i in (0...n).to_a.reverse
        if i == n - 1
            r_greater[i] = true
        else
            if nums[i] > current_max
                r_greater[i] = true
            end
        end
        current_max = [current_max, nums[i]].max
    end

    result = []
    for i in 0...n
        # An element is valid if it satisfies the left condition OR the right condition.
        # Since L[0]=T and R[n-1]=T, the boundary conditions are covered.
        if l_greater[i] || r_greater[i]
            result << nums[i]
        end
    end

    return result
end
